matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs2234693 (ESR1 PvuII marker) (ESR1 intronic association marker)
ESR1 ligand-binding-domain resistance model (ESR1 endocrine-resistance model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a ESR1 steroid hormone receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to estrogen receptor transcriptional control, chromatin binding, endocrine signaling, and anti-estrogen response biology and Endocrine-responsive breast cancer context, Estrogen signaling and reproductive tissue biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

This specific marker is curated as ESR1 intronic association marker. rs2234693 (ESR1 PvuII marker) is curated as a ESR1 marker for Endocrine-responsive breast cancer context, Estrogen signaling and reproductive tissue biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the estrogen receptor transcriptional control, chromatin binding, endocrine signaling, and anti-estrogen response biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs2234693 (ESR1 PvuII marker) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the ESR1 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a ESR1 steroid hormone receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to estrogen receptor transcriptional control, chromatin binding, endocrine signaling, and anti-estrogen response biology and Endocrine-responsive breast cancer context, Estrogen signaling and reproductive tissue biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed ESR1 variant places this sample in the curated ESR1 research context. The local ESR1 bundle is steroid hormone receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. ESR1 variants can alter or tag estrogen receptor transcriptional control, chromatin binding, endocrine signaling, and anti-estrogen response biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta

This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus.
